vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2054: Close-out is not terminal — closed/closed-out issues linger in the pipeline

plan.title: Make close-out terminal: stop closed/closed-out issues lingering in the pipeline
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: A closed + closed-out issue keeps appearing in the Command Deck as active pipeline work because close-out is not terminal: it leaves (1) a paused agent's live tmux session [AC1 already fixed in resource-discovery.ts], (2) workspace + feature branch + agent-state on disk, (3) a stale durable `pipeline` journal block that getReviewStatusSync re-derives an active state from, and (4) non-terminal labels `merged`/`ready` that close-out never strips. Repro: PAN-1832 lingered for hours and its agent-state dir still exists.
narratives.Proposal: Make close-out terminal at the source of truth. A) Mark the durable record's pipeline block terminal with a new `closedOut` flag and short-circuit getReviewStatusSync to null for closed-out issues so no active state can be re-derived. B) Strip `merged`/`ready` at close-out via a new terminal-only label list. C) Add an idempotent, merge-guarded deacon reaper that removes residue (tmux session, workspace worktree, merged feature branch local+remote, agent-state dir) for closed issues — including the pure-disk case with no running agent — reusing isBranchMerged so unmerged work is never destroyed. D) Lock the behavior with tests, including a regression test for the already-shipped resource-tree exclusion (FR-5).
narratives.NonGoals:
- Refactoring close-out's existing inline teardown (steps 3/5) to call the new reapIssueResidue helper (dedup deferred).
- Changing postMergeLifecycle's decision to pause the work agent at merge.
- Any change to resource-discovery filtering beyond a regression-lock test (FR-5 already shipped).
- Adding `merged`/`ready` to WORKFLOW_LABELS (would affect other in-flight callers).
- Manual one-off scripts to delete PAN-1832 residue (the new patrol does it on first run).
- SQLite schema changes or any new direct store access outside the two doors.

autoDecisions.summary: Use an explicit `closedOut`/`closedOutAt` flag on the pipeline record + getReviewStatusSync short-circuit, not merely mergeStatus='merged'.
autoDecisions.rationale: A dedicated terminal flag is robust against future reviewGatesPassedSync changes and gives the read door an unambiguous 'not active' signal; the issue explicitly allows 'reset to terminal (or clear it)'.

autoDecisions.summary: Reaping lives in the extended deacon reaper patrol (idempotent, merge-guarded), not only in a one-shot close-out step.
autoDecisions.rationale: PAN-1832's residue survived because an agent reappeared AFTER close-out ran; only a re-running patrol catches that. It also auto-reaps PAN-1832 on first run with no manual step.

autoDecisions.summary: Strip `merged`/`ready` via a new separate POST_MERGE_RESIDUE_LABELS const, not by extending WORKFLOW_LABELS.
autoDecisions.rationale: WORKFLOW_LABELS is documented as current-phase labels and shared by other close paths; merged/ready are post-merge/backlog labels that in-flight callers legitimately keep.

autoDecisions.summary: Leave close-out's inline teardown (steps 3/5) unchanged; add reapIssueResidue additively.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps beads surgical and avoids destabilizing the working close-out path; unification is a deferred NonGoal.

autoDecisions.summary: PAN-1832's specific disk residue is reaped by the new patrol on first run; no manual teardown bead.
autoDecisions.rationale: It is closed + merged, so the merge guard passes and the disk scan finds it.

items.title: Add `closedOut`/`closedOutAt` to the pipeline record + write-door marker

narrative.Action: In src/lib/pan-dir/record.ts: add optional `closedOut?: boolean` and `closedOutAt?: string` to interface PanIssuePipelineRecord (record.ts:60). Add a write-door helper `markRecordPipelineClosedOutSync(project, issueId)` modeled exactly on clearRecordFeedbackSync (record.ts:520): ensureIssueRecordSync → set record.pipeline.closedOut=true, record.pipeline.closedOutAt=<ISO now>, record.pipeline.readyForMerge=false, record.pipeline.verificationStatus=undefined, record.pipeline.mergeStatus='merged', record.pipeline.updatedAt=<ISO now> → writeIssueRecordSync → queueIssueRecordCommit. Ensure projectPipeline (records.ts:83) preserves closedOut/closedOutAt when rebuilding from an existing record so a later updateIssueRecordForIssue does not drop the flag. Re-export markRecordPipelineClosedOutSync from src/lib/pan-dir/records.ts. No direct file/DB access outside writeIssueRecordSync.

items.title: Given a PanIssuePipelineRecord, the type accepts `closedOut: true` and `closedOutAt: <ISO string>` and the project typechecks (npm run typecheck passes).

Calling markRecordPipelineClosedOutSync(project, issueId) persists pipeline.closedOut=true, closedOutAt set, readyForMerge=false, verificationStatus=undefined, mergeStatus='merged' to <projectRoot>/.pan/records/<issue>.json (verified by reading the record back in a unit test).
After markRecordPipelineClosedOutSync, a subsequent updateIssueRecordForIssue(issueId, status) preserves closedOut=true (projectPipeline carries the flag forward).

markRecordPipelineClosedOutSync writes only via writeIssueRecordSync/queueIssueRecordCommit — no new direct fs.writeFile to .pan/records or SQLite (verified by code inspection / the single-source-of-truth CI guard staying green).
getReviewStatusSync returns terminal (null) for a closed-out journal

narrative.Action: Surface closedOut/closedOutAt in readJournalStatusSync.durable (src/lib/overdeck/review-status-record-sync.ts:57). In getReviewStatusSync (src/lib/review-status.ts:707): immediately after `const journal = readJournalStatusSync(issueId)`, if journal?.durable.closedOut === true, best-effort dbDelete(issueId) and `return null` — BEFORE the `journalNewer` reconcile block (hazard H1), so a stale verificationStatus:'running' can never be re-derived. Add a short comment citing PAN-2054.

items.title: Given a journal with pipeline.closedOut=true and a stale durable verificationStatus:'running', getReviewStatusSync(issueId) returns null (unit test).
Given a journal WITHOUT closedOut (normal in-flight issue), getReviewStatusSync still reconciles and returns the active ReviewStatus exactly as before (existing review-status tests stay green).
readJournalStatusSync(issueId).durable includes closedOut and closedOutAt when present in the record's pipeline block (unit test against review-status-record-sync).
close-out marks the pipeline journal terminal

narrative.Action: In src/lib/close-out.ts executeCloseOutPromise, add a step alongside step 8 ('Clear review status', close-out.ts:507) that calls markRecordPipelineClosedOutSync for ctx.issueId. Order: mark the journal terminal FIRST, then clearReviewStatus(DB), so a concurrent read can never observe DB-cleared-but-journal-active. Resolve the ProjectConfig from ctx (close-out already has projectPath; use getProjectConfigFromWorkspacePath/resolveProjectForIssue). Push a CloseOutStep {name:'Mark pipeline terminal', status:'passed'|'skipped'} like the other steps; non-fatal on error (skipped + warning), matching step 8's tolerance.

items.title: After executeCloseOutPromise succeeds for an issue, its .pan/records/<issue>.json pipeline block has closedOut=true and readyForMerge=false (integration assertion in close-out test).
The close-out steps array contains a 'Mark pipeline terminal' step that passes on the happy path.
If markRecordPipelineClosedOutSync throws, close-out still returns success:true with the step recorded as 'skipped' (close-out is not aborted by the marker failing).
close-out strips `merged` and `ready` labels

narrative.Action: Export a new const POST_MERGE_RESIDUE_LABELS = ['merged', 'ready'] in src/lib/lifecycle/close-issue.ts next to WORKFLOW_LABELS (close-issue.ts:35), kept SEPARATE from WORKFLOW_LABELS. In close-out.ts step 7 (close-out.ts:452-458), after the existing WORKFLOW_LABELS removal loop, remove each POST_MERGE_RESIDUE_LABELS entry for both the GitHub path (gh issue edit --remove-label "<label>" 2>/dev/null || true) and the Linear path (drop the matching labelIds). Keep present-label tolerance.

items.title: A unit test asserts POST_MERGE_RESIDUE_LABELS contains exactly 'merged' and 'ready', and that WORKFLOW_LABELS preserves its original entries (unchanged).

Given a GitHub issue carrying `merged` and `ready`, close-out's label step issues `gh issue edit --remove-label merged` and `--remove-label ready` (verified via a mocked exec asserting both remove-label calls fire).
An issue lacking `merged`/`ready` still passes close-out's label step (the `|| true` tolerance means absent labels do not fail the step).
Idempotent, merge-guarded reapIssueResidue helper

narrative.Action: New module src/lib/cloister/reap-issue-residue.ts exporting `reapIssueResidue(projectPath: string, issueId: string): Promise<string[]>`, idempotent and merge-guarded. Steps, each best-effort and each appending an action string: (a) kill tmux sessions agent-<issue>, test-<issue>, merge-<issue>, and review-<issue>-<ts>-* via Effect killSession (mirror close-out.ts:269-290). (b) MERGE GUARD: call isBranchMerged('feature/<issue>', projectPath) (import/reuse from close-out.ts:38) — proceed to disk/branch deletion ONLY when status is 'merged' or 'no-branch'; on 'unmerged' append 'skipped reap for <issue> — branch unmerged' and return immediately. (c) git worktree remove <workspacePath> --force via execAsync, fallback rmSync (mirror close-out.ts:300-311). (d) delete local (git branch -D) and remote (git push origin --delete) feature/<issue>. (e) rmSync ~/.overdeck/agents/agent-<issue> and planning-<issue> (AGENTS_DIR from paths.js). Use execAsync + Effect killSession ONLY — never execSync (no-execsync rule). Re-running on already-clean residue is a no-op that returns [].

items.title: Given a closed issue with a MERGED feature branch + workspace dir + agent-state dir, reapIssueResidue removes the worktree, deletes the local feature/<issue> branch, and removes ~/.overdeck/agents/agent-<issue> (verified in a tmp-fixture unit test); it returns a non-empty action list.
Given an UNMERGED feature branch, reapIssueResidue deletes NOTHING on disk and returns an action containing 'skipped' and 'unmerged' (merge-guard test, NFR-1).
Calling reapIssueResidue twice in a row is idempotent: the second call finds no residue and returns an empty action list without throwing.
reapIssueResidue contains no execSync call (uses execAsync + Effect killSession); grep for execSync in the new module returns nothing.
Wire reapIssueResidue into the closed-issue reaper patrol

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/closed-issue-reaper.ts: (1) After the existing agent/session stop loops in BOTH handleIssueStatusChangedClosed (closed-issue-reaper.ts:35) and reconcileClosedIssueAgents (:77), call reapIssueResidue(projectPath, issueId) for each CLOSED issue and append its actions. Resolve projectPath via resolveProjectForIssue/getProjectConfigFromWorkspacePath. (2) Add a pure-disk scan in reconcileClosedIssueAgents (hazard H4/H5): enumerate <projectRoot>/workspaces/feature-* dirs and ~/.overdeck/agents/agent-* dirs across configured projects, map each to an issue id, and for ids that isIssueClosed() returns true, call reapIssueResidue (the internal merge guard keeps it safe). Keep the existing `if (getNoResumeMode().active) return []` early-return at the top (NFR-3). Dedup so an issue already reaped via its running agent is not scanned again.

items.title: Given a closed issue whose branch is merged and which has NO running agent (pure-disk residue), one reconcileClosedIssueAgents cycle calls reapIssueResidue for it and its workspace/branch/agent-state are removed (test with mocked listRunningAgents=[] + a disk fixture).
When getNoResumeMode().active is true, reconcileClosedIssueAgents returns [] and reapIssueResidue is never called (test asserts the mock is not invoked).
Given an OPEN (not closed) issue with a workspace on disk, the disk scan preserves it — reapIssueResidue is not called (isIssueClosed gate).
The existing closed-issue-reaper.test.ts suite passes after the change (agents/sessions are still stopped for closed issues).
Integration test: close-out leaves no re-derivable active state

narrative.Action: Add an integration test (e.g. src/lib/__tests__/close-out-terminal.test.ts or extend review-status-record-sync.test.ts) that reproduces PAN-1832's state: seed a per-issue record whose pipeline block is stale-active (verificationStatus:'running', mergeStatus:'pending'), call markRecordPipelineClosedOutSync, then clearReviewStatus, then assert getReviewStatusSync(issueId) returns null AND the record's pipeline.closedOut===true. Use tmp fixtures; do not hit the network or the real ~/.overdeck. Follow fake-timers rules only if any delay is involved (none expected here).

items.title: The test seeds a stale-active journal, runs markRecordPipelineClosedOutSync + clearReviewStatus, and asserts getReviewStatusSync returns null (re-derivation prevented).
The test confirms the durable record persists pipeline.closedOut===true and readyForMerge===false after the sequence.
The test passes under `npm test` without requiring maxForks=1 (no real timers / no real network).
Regression test: terminal+closed-out issue excluded from the resource tree (FR-5)

narrative.Action: In tests/unit/dashboard/server/services/resource-discovery.test.ts, add a regression test locking the already-shipped exclusion (resource-discovery.ts ~:635-665): a CLOSED + closed-out issue with NO open PR is excluded from the resource-allocated tree; the SAME issue WITH an open PR is retained. If an equivalent assertion already exists, extend it to cover both the no-PR (excluded) and open-PR (retained) branches rather than duplicating.

items.title: Given a CLOSED issue with trackerState='closed' and no open PR, the resource-discovery result does NOT include it (test).
Given the same CLOSED issue but WITH an open PR, the resource-discovery result DOES include it (open-PR exception retained).
